title: Trust Architecture

introduction.subtitle: How do you build, verify, and lose trust? A blueprint of five trust mechanisms.
introduction.description:
Trust is not one thing — you build it through different mechanisms in different contexts. This maps which mechanisms you rely on: relational bonds, written contracts, reputation, transparent process, or shared governance. None is universally correct; each has a context where it works and one where it fails.

Pick the option closest to what you would actually do, not what sounds most virtuous.

Use the result as language for reflection and conversation, not as a permanent label. The terms are meant to make patterns easier to notice, compare, and discuss.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is your choice.
introduction.disclaimer: Original instrument. This is not a validated psychometric assessment.

questions.text: Before signing a long partnership, what makes you most willing to move forward?

options.text: I need to actually know and trust the person across the table.
I need clear written terms covering what happens if things go wrong.
I need strong references and a track record I can verify.
I need a transparent process with audit and review milestones.
I need to see shared governance and mutual accountability between our groups.

conceptHelp.title: Trust mechanism: Before signing a long partnership, what makes you most willing to move forward
conceptHelp.summary: A trust mechanism is the signal you rely on before depending on someone: personal relationship, contract, reputation, transparent process, or shared community accountability. This question asks which mechanism would carry the most weight in the situation "Before signing a long partnership, what makes you most willing to move forward?".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Different partnerships need different trust mechanisms. The result helps you notice when your default fits the situation and when it may leave a blind spot.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Think about what would actually make you proceed in a consequential relationship, not what sounds morally best in theory.
conceptHelp.example: The options contrast I need to actually know and trust the person across the table. / I need clear written terms covering what happens if things go wrong. / I need strong references and a track record I can verify.; your answer shows which trust signal you would prioritize first.

questions.text: A new collaborator with strong credentials joins. What is your first move?

options.text: Get to know them in person and build the bond early.
Draft a scope and agreement that defines the relationship.
Talk to people who have worked with them before.
Set up the checks and review cadence they will work under.
Plug them into a shared governance structure rather than a private channel.

conceptHelp.title: Trust mechanism: A new collaborator with strong credentials joins. What is your first move
conceptHelp.summary: A trust mechanism is the signal you rely on before depending on someone: personal relationship, contract, reputation, transparent process, or shared community accountability. This question asks which mechanism would carry the most weight in the situation "A new collaborator with strong credentials joins. What is your first move?".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Different partnerships need different trust mechanisms. The result helps you notice when your default fits the situation and when it may leave a blind spot.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Think about what would actually make you proceed in a consequential relationship, not what sounds morally best in theory.
conceptHelp.example: The options contrast Get to know them in person and build the bond early. / Draft a scope and agreement that defines the relationship. / Talk to people who have worked with them before.; your answer shows which trust signal you would prioritize first.

questions.text: Someone broke a commitment they made to you. What changes your willingness to keep working with them?

options.text: Whether the relationship and good faith are still there.
Whether the agreement has teeth and the breach is documentable.
Whether their breach becomes visible enough to cost them.
Whether there is a clear process to escalate and repair.
Whether the group they belong to holds them accountable.

conceptHelp.title: Trust mechanism: Someone broke a commitment they made to you. What changes your willingness to keep working with them
conceptHelp.summary: A trust mechanism is the signal you rely on before depending on someone: personal relationship, contract, reputation, transparent process, or shared community accountability. This question asks which mechanism would carry the most weight in the situation "Someone broke a commitment they made to you. What changes your willingness to keep working with them?".

conceptHelp.example: The options contrast Whether the relationship and good faith are still there. / Whether the agreement has teeth and the breach is documentable. / Whether their breach becomes visible enough to cost them.; your answer shows which trust signal you would prioritize first.

questions.text: You must decide whether to trust a supplier you have never used.

options.text: I meet the owner and trust my read of them.
I sign a tight contract with real penalties.
I check references and past performance with their customers.
I require transparency into their process and quality controls.
I check whether they are accountable to a cooperative or shared standard.

This question asks which mechanism would carry the most weight in the situation "You must decide whether to trust a supplier you have never used.".

questions.text: How do you most reliably verify a claim someone makes to you?

options.text: Through whether I trust the person making it.
Through a written record and agreed definitions.
Through independent references and their track record.
Through an audit or a verifiable procedure.
Through whether the group they belong to vouches for it.

conceptHelp.title: Trust mechanism: How do you most reliably verify a claim someone makes to you
conceptHelp.summary: A trust mechanism is the signal you rely on before depending on someone: personal relationship, contract, reputation, transparent process, or shared community accountability. This question asks which mechanism would carry the most weight in the situation "How do you most reliably verify a claim someone makes to you?".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Different partnerships need different trust mechanisms. The result helps you notice when your default fits the situation and when it may leave a blind spot.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Think about what would actually make you proceed in a consequential relationship, not what sounds morally best in theory.
conceptHelp.example: The options contrast Through whether I trust the person making it. / Through a written record and agreed definitions. / Through independent references and their track record.; your answer shows which trust signal you would prioritize first.

questions.text: A funder offers support. What makes you accept it?

options.text: A real relationship, not just a transaction.
Clear terms on what the money can and cannot be used for.
Their reputation and the signal their support sends.
Transparent reporting requirements and milestones.
Shared governance over how the funds affect the mission.

conceptHelp.title: Trust mechanism: A funder offers support. What makes you accept it
conceptHelp.summary: A trust mechanism is the signal you rely on before depending on someone: personal relationship, contract, reputation, transparent process, or shared community accountability. This question asks which mechanism would carry the most weight in the situation "A funder offers support. What makes you accept it?".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Different partnerships need different trust mechanisms. The result helps you notice when your default fits the situation and when it may leave a blind spot.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Think about what would actually make you proceed in a consequential relationship, not what sounds morally best in theory.
conceptHelp.example: The options contrast A real relationship, not just a transaction. / Clear terms on what the money can and cannot be used for. / Their reputation and the signal their support sends.; your answer shows which trust signal you would prioritize first.

questions.text: A small group inside your venture starts doing work no one else can see. What matters most?

options.text: That the people are trusted enough not to need watching.
That there is a written agreement defining their autonomy.
That they have a results track record that speaks for them.
That the work is auditable and the process is visible.
That the work is accountable to the group, not private islands.

questions.text: You are choosing between two equal bids. What tips your decision?

options.text: Which people I would rather work with.
Which contract terms best protect us.
Which bidder has the stronger track record.
Whose process is most transparent and auditable.
Whose structure best fits shared accountability.

conceptHelp.title: Trust mechanism: You are choosing between two equal bids. What tips your decision
conceptHelp.summary: A trust mechanism is the signal you rely on before depending on someone: personal relationship, contract, reputation, transparent process, or shared community accountability. This question asks which mechanism would carry the most weight in the situation "You are choosing between two equal bids. What tips your decision?".

conceptHelp.example: The options contrast Which people I would rather work with. / Which contract terms best protect us. / Which bidder has the stronger track record.; your answer shows which trust signal you would prioritize first.

questions.text: When things go wrong with a partner, what do you rely on most?

options.text: The relationship to absorb the friction.
The contract to define the resolution.
Reputational consequences to keep them honest.
A process we agreed in advance.
The shared group to mediate and hold them accountable.

conceptHelp.title: Trust mechanism: When things go wrong with a partner, what do you rely on most
conceptHelp.summary: A trust mechanism is the signal you rely on before depending on someone: personal relationship, contract, reputation, transparent process, or shared community accountability. questions.text: Trust scales best when, in your view, it is built on...

options.text: Real, repeated human bonds.
Clear written agreements.
Track records that anyone can verify.
Transparent processes anyone can audit.
Shared governance and collective ownership.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
